headline: secrets-guard branch alignment + ssh-config registry entry

bullets: secrets-guard.sh: standalone main() Read and Grep handlers now route through their existing match/check pairs (the Bash branch and dispatched paths already did). Pre-cleanup the Read pair was bypassed by inline logic and the Grep pair was orphaned with no caller at all.

Surfaced and closed a superset-invariant violation on the Read branch: ~/.ssh/config was admitted by check (via an explicit fallback) but not by match. Promoted ssh-config to a first-class detection-registry.json entry (anchored pattern \.ssh/config$ to preserve exact-match behavior) so the predicate and the check derive from the same alternation.

Behavior unchanged for consumers in normal use — ~/.ssh/config always blocked in standalone mode and always blocked in dispatched mode; both modes are now uniformly aligned. 89/89 secrets-guard Shape B tests continue to pass.
